reasoning: Peer set (LEFT) is 12 helmet/armored-head enemy sprites rendered in a cool, desaturated metallic pixel-art style: grays, silvers, gold accents, hard specular highlights, tight dark outlines, and a neutral-to-slightly-menacing charm tone. Candidate (RIGHT) breaks on nearly every stylistic axis: (1) OUTLINE WEIGHT — peers carry a consistent 1px dark outer stroke; candidate outline appears marginally thinner on organic curves, borderline acceptable. (2) OUTLINE COLOR — peers use near-black; candidate uses dark brown/black mix, minor drift. (3) SHADOW DIRECTION — candidate and peers both suggest top-left lighting, consistent. (4) SHADOW COUNT — both use single layered shadow, consistent. (5) HIGHLIGHT INTENSITY — peers show hard metallic specular dots; candidate shows soft diffuse highlights on shell and skin, significant style divergence. (6) COLOR TEMPERATURE — CRITICAL FAIL: peers are uniformly cool (steel grays, slate blues, muted golds); candidate is strongly warm (orange body, cream highlight, teal accents). No temperature overlap. (7) DETAIL DENSITY — peers have intricate engraved/riveted helmet geometry; candidate is rounded with fewer surface details, noticeably lower density. (8) PROPORTION — peers are compact head-centric silhouettes; candidate is a chibi character with visible torso/arms; acceptable as different enemy archetype. (9) RENDERING STYLE — peers are hard-surface metallic renders; candidate is soft organic creature rendering, fundamentally different material language. (10) PALETTE OVERLAP — virtually zero: peer palette is steel/silver/charcoal/crimson accent; candidate palette is orange/teal/white/warm-brown — no shared hues. (11) CHARM LEVEL — peers read as dangerous armored constructs with zero cuteness; candidate is overtly cute (large round eyes, bubbly silhouette, warm colors) — charm mismatch is severe and would visually break enemy family cohesion. Overall: 5 of 11 dimensions are failing or critically inconsistent. The candidate feels like it belongs to a different game's art style.
